item.title: Autism risk if toxins, heavy metals, microplastics, etc during pregnancy (unless add Vitamin D)
item.description:
• The Autism Spectrum Disorder and Its Possible Origins in Pregnancy
      • VitaminDWiki – Microplastics now causing problems in most life forms - many studies

The Autism Spectrum Disorder and Its Possible Origins in Pregnancy

Int. J. Environ. Res. Public Health 2024, 21(3), 244; https://doi.org/10.3390/ijerph21030244

Rayane M. Botelho ORCID,Ana Lúcia M. Silva ORCID and Alexandre U. Borbely *ORCID

item.title: 90% in US have chlormequat chloride in urine – which is linked to reduced fertility

Americans Exposed to Fertility-Lowering Chemicals in Cheerios, Quaker Oats

Mercola   PDF

92% of (non-ogranic) oat-based foods, including popular brands like Quaker Oats and Cheerios, also contained the chemical

EPA Considers Expanding Chlormequat (a pesticide) Use in Crops
Date %
2017 69%
2018-2022 74%
2023 90%

VitaminDWiki - Pesticides 8 X less likely to result in female infertility if take vitamin D – Sept 2023

VitaminDWiki – Pesticides increase risk of Cancers, Alz, ALS, Asthma, ADHD, etc. (all related to low vitamin D) – Oct 2016

item.title: Beans etc, can cut in half the response to fat-soluble Vitamin D and K
item.description:
• The Presence of Pulses within a Meal can Alter Fat-Soluble Vitamin Bioavailability
      • Can bypass both poor gut and pulse limitations with non-oral Vitamin supplementation
      • Note: Response to water-soluble form of Vitamin D is not known to be reduced by pulses

The Presence of Pulses within a Meal can Alter Fat-Soluble Vitamin Bioavailability

Molecular Nutrition and Food Research, 2019, 63 (11), pp.1801323. ff10.1002/mnfr.201801323ff

Marielle Margier, Tiffany Antoine, Aurélie Siriaco, Marion Nowicki, Charlotte Halimi, Matthieu Maillot, Stéphane Georgé, Emmanuelle Reboul

item.title: Fructose, HFCS and Vitamin D - many studies
item.description:
Both Fructose and HFCS (High Fructose Corn Syrup) reduce Vitamin D

      both the tested and fully activated forms of Vitamin D

Vitamin D supplementation reduces problems resulting from Fructose or HFCS

      • Vitamin D supplement reduced some HFCS problems in rats - Feb 2024
      • Vitamin D supplementation reduced problems of fructose-rich diets in mice - Sept 2019
      • Scientists claim to have found root cause of obesity for most people (HFCS) - Oct 2023
      • Fructose drives de novo lipogenesis affecting metabolic health - March 2023

Vitamin D supplement reduced some HFCS problems in rats - Feb 2024

Protective effect of vitamin D on learning and memory impairment in rats induced by high fructose corn syrup

item.title: Pesticides 8 X less likely to result in female infertility if take vitamin D
item.description:
• Vitamin D Intake Attenuated the Association between Pesticides Exposure and Female Infertility
      • 4 Other pesticide and Vitamin D studies in VitaminDWiki
      • Search for (pesticides OR DDT) Vitamin D: 223,000 studies in Google Scholar

Vitamin D Intake Attenuated the Association between Pesticides Exposure and Female Infertility

Clin Lab. 2023 Sep 1;69(9). doi: 10.7754/Clin.Lab.2023.230201.

Chunxiu Wu, Xiaoqin Xin, Jianlan Chen

Background: Only a few epidemiological studies have illuminated the association between pesticide exposure and female infertility. However, evidence of the available data is restricted and also controversial. Vitamin D supplement was considered as having a beneficial effect on fertility. So, the purpose of our study is to assess the effect of dietary vitamin D consumption on the relationship between pesticide exposure in home and female infertility.

item.title: Fluoride reduces Vitamin D and Magnesium - many studies
item.description:
• Far more vitamin D deficiency if high fluoride in water - Jan 2021

Far more vitamin D deficiency if high fluoride in water - Jan 2021

<10 ng/ml Of Vitamin D in pregnant mother’s blood
Low fluoride 44% of mothers
High fluoride 61% of mothers

The association of fluoride in drinking water with serum calcium, vitamin D and parathyroid hormone in pregnant women and newborn infants

https://doi.org/10.1038/s41430-020-00707-2

Download the PDF from Sci-Hub via VitaminDWiki

item.title: Parkinson's Disease 5X more likely if Trichloroethylene (TCE, wonder about Vit D)
item.description:
• Trichloroethylene: An Invisible Cause of Parkinson’s Disease?
      • Review of study includes other research by Dr. Dorsey, principal author - March 2023
      • Comments on this study and how to avoid TCE - April 2023

Trichloroethylene: An Invisible Cause of Parkinson’s Disease?

Journal of Parkinson’s Disease, vol. 13, no. 2, pp. 203-218, 2023 DOI: 10.3233/JPD-225047

Dorsey , E. Raya; b; 1; * | Zafar, Maryama; 1 | Lettenberger, Samantha E.a | Pawlik, Meghan E.a | Kinel, Dana; b | Frissen, Myrthec | Schneider, Ruth B.a; b | Kieburtz, Karla; b | Tanner, Caroline M.d | De Miranda, Briana R.e | Goldman, Samuel M.f | Bloem, Bastiaan R.c

item.title: Fluoride lowers IQ in Children (It blocks Vitamin D and Magnesium)
item.description:
Report Linking Fluoride to Lower IQ in Children Made Public After CDC, HHS Tried to Block It

The National Toxicology Program on Wednesday released a draft report linking prenatal and childhood fluoride exposure to reduced IQ in children, after public health officials tried for almost a year to block its publication.

By Brenda Baletti, Ph.D.

https://childrenshealthdefense.org/defender/ntp-report-fluoride-lower-iq-children/

The National Toxicology Program (NTP) on Wednesday released a draft report linking prenatal and childhood fluoride exposure to reduced IQ in children, after public health officials tried for almost a year to block its publication.

item.title: Allergic rhinitis sublingual therapy was 22X more successful if good level of Vitamin D
item.description:
• Serum vitamin D3 deficiency can affect the efficacy of sublingual immunotherapy in children with allergic rhinitis: a retrospective cohort study

Serum vitamin D3 deficiency can affect the efficacy of sublingual immunotherapy in children with allergic rhinitis: a retrospective cohort study

J Thorac Dis . 2023 Feb 28;15(2):649-657. doi: 10.21037/jtd-22-1883. Epub 2023 Feb 22.

Liang Li # 1, Xiaojian Cui # 2, Xinjie Zhang # 3 4, Lisheng Zheng 2, Xuyuan Sun 1, Cuihong Yang 1, Jianbo Shu 3 4, Guangping Liu 1

item.title: High fluoride in water: 5X more likely to be Vitamin D deficient
item.description:
• Vitamin D deficiency in patients with diabetes and its correlation with water fluoride levels
      • High Fluoride concentations in ground water in areas with Vitamin D deficiency
      • Fluoride in Volcanic Areas: A Case Study in Medical Geology- May 2019
      • Global analysis and prediction of fluoride in groundwater - 2022
      • Fluoride in natural waters - 2013
      • Excess fluoridation reduces Magnesium, and causes health problems

Vitamin D deficiency in patients with diabetes and its correlation with water fluoride levels

J Water Health . 2023 Jan;21(1):125-137. doi: 10.2166/wh.2022.254.
